811.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Porsche tribute that never found its footing competitively.

_comment: Supplementary/corrective vehicle data. Auto-bootstrapped from GTABase cache + manually maintained. Applied on top of automated sync pipeline.

_format._description: Supplementary/corrective data layer for gta-car-checklist. Merges on top of the automated sync pipeline. Can correct wrong data from automated sources AND fill gaps with data no automated source has.
_format._key: Vehicle display name (must match DurtyFree display name exactly). Use DurtyFree hash as _hash field for disambiguation if needed.

_merge_strategy.scalars: Replacement — field value overwrites automated source value
_merge_strategy.arrays_add: Fields ending in _add are additive — values are merged with automated source array
_merge_strategy.arrays_remove: Fields ending in _remove subtract from automated source array
_merge_strategy.arrays_replace: Fields without _add/_remove suffix replace the automated source array entirely

_metadata_fields._added: ISO date this entry was created (YYYY-MM-DD)
_metadata_fields._reason: Why this entry exists — what was wrong or missing in the automated source
_metadata_fields._source_issue: Which automated source had the problem (e.g. 'GTABase', 'DurtyFree')
_metadata_fields._missing_from_durtyfree: Boolean — true if this vehicle is absent from DurtyFree data
_metadata_fields._review_after: ISO date — flag this entry for review (e.g. after a game update that may have fixed upstream data)

_data_fields.price: Integer — standard buy price in GTA$
_data_fields.trade_price: Integer — reduced trade price in GTA$ (if applicable)
_data_fields.trade_price_condition: String — human-readable unlock condition for trade price
_data_fields.stores: Array of store names — REPLACES automated source (use stores_add for additive)
_data_fields.stores_add: Array of store names to ADD to automated source
_data_fields.stores_remove: Array of store names to REMOVE from automated source
_data_fields.features: Array of feature strings — REPLACES automated source (use features_add for additive)
_data_fields.features_add: Array of features to ADD to automated source
_data_fields.features_remove: Array of features to REMOVE from automated source
_data_fields.unlock_methods: Array of unlock type strings. Valid values: 'purchase', 'trade_price', 'lucky_wheel', 'returning_player', 'prime_gaming', 'completion_reward', 'research_unlock', 'cannot_acquire', 'expired'
_data_fields.unlock_condition_note: String — human-readable explanation of unlock condition for display
_data_fields.use_case: Array of use case tags. Valid values: 'racing', 'combat', 'off-road', 'transport', 'utility', 'grinding', 'collector', 'fun'. Vehicles can have multiple tags. 'grinding' = optimized for missions/heists/passive income. 'fun' = beloved/enjoyable vehicles that aren't competitively meta. 'collector' = rare/status/aesthetic.
_data_fields.racing_tier: String or null. Broughy1322's tier scale (S, A–O, or null). S=best in class/race-meta, descending through O=slowest, null=not a racing vehicle.
_data_fields.racing_lap_time: Float or null — tested lap time in seconds (source: Broughy1322). null if not applicable.
_data_fields.grinding_tier: String or null. Grinding meta tier: S=best grind vehicle, A/B/C=diminishing value, skip=not worth it, null=not a grind vehicle.
_data_fields.meta_verdict: String or null. Overall vehicle verdict: must_buy, situational, outclassed, fun_only, niche, or null.
_data_fields.meta_note: String or null. Short explanation for meta_verdict (1-2 sentences max).
_data_fields.storage_type: String or null. Where the vehicle is stored: garage, pegasus, or null (unknown/property-specific).
_data_fields.removed_from_stores: Boolean — true if vehicle was removed from in-game stores and can no longer be purchased.

Nightshark._reason: GTABase missing Missile Lock-On Jammer feature

Ocelot Pariah._reason: Adding use_case and racing data not present in automated sources

Oppressor Mk II._reason: Adding use_case and unlock method detail
Oppressor Mk II.unlock_condition_note: Trade price unlocked via Nightclub Terrorbyte research

Itali Classic.meta_verdict: Pricey Itali Classic at $2.025M with no racing benchmark — Italian heritage at a steep premium.

Astrale.meta_verdict: Stylish Sport Classic with no racing benchmark yet — an aesthetic purchase until the data arrives.

Luiva.meta_verdict: Recent addition still finding its place; early data suggests solid mid-tier at best.

GT750.meta_verdict: Sport Classic with no racing tier benchmarked — buy it for the aesthetics while the data catches up.

FMJ MK V.meta_verdict: Weaponized FMJ update; niche combat super for players who want missiles and speed.

Drift Walton L35.meta_verdict: Drift-spec Walton L35 — off-road truck built to slide; fun niche toy with limited practical application.

Rapid GT X.meta_verdict: C-tier successor to the iconic Rapid GT; competent but expensive for what the tier delivers.

Sentinel GTS.meta_verdict: C-tier BMW homage with clean handling; wins races without demanding attention.

LSCM Cheetah Classic.meta_verdict: LSCM Cheetah Classic — A-tier performance at double the base price; Car Meet exclusivity tax.

Suzume.meta_verdict: C-tier newcomer at $3.1M — competitive but expensive for what the tier delivers.

Tampa GT.meta_verdict: E-tier Tampa variant at $1.31M — the GT suffix promises more than the racing tier delivers.

Woodlander.meta_verdict: C-tier Woodlander — capable mid-range SUV that sits comfortably in the competitive middle of the class.

Everon RS.meta_verdict: S-tier Everon RS — top-flight SUV racer with the performance to match its premium position.

Caracara Pursuit.unlock_condition_note: Straight Warstock purchase, no property gate. Owning one enables Dispatch Work missions. Trade price ($4,001,250) unlocks after completing "Slush Fund" as mission leader AND "Dispatch Work - Obstruction of Justice".

Uranus LozSpeed.meta_verdict: A-tier Sport Classic with genuine racing credentials — one of the class's newer competitive additions.

Firebolt ASP.unlock_condition_note: Purchasable at SSASA on all platforms. Free alternate unlock (E&E/PS5/Series X only): complete Tier 4 Agents of Sabotage Career Progress challenges.
Firebolt ASP._note: E&E-exclusive free path exists alongside standard purchase.
Firebolt ASP.meta_verdict: C-tier Firebolt ASP — high price for a mid-pack off-road result; the ASP suffix overpromises.

Terminus Patrol.unlock_condition_note: Straight Warstock purchase. Trade price ($3,843,750) unlocks after completing "Slush Fund" as leader AND "Dispatch Work - Health Code Violations".

Coquette D5.meta_verdict: B-tier at $1.73M — one of the better value propositions in the competitive sports tier.

Banshee GTS.meta_verdict: B-tier debut from Agents of Sabotage; immediately competitive and a genuine Pariah alternative.

Dominator FX.meta_verdict: S-tier Dominator variant with event livery — competes at the top with style to match.

Pizza Boy.meta_verdict: Delivery moped at $195K — GTA Online's most expensive joke purchase.

Castigator.meta_verdict: S-tier Castigator joins the SUV elite — competitive from the moment it launched.

Yosemite 1500.meta_verdict: F-tier lifted Yosemite 1500 at $1.205M — the truck aesthetic doesn't justify the price.

Envisage.meta_verdict: B-tier Jaguar E-Type tribute with genuine racing credentials; the price is the only hesitation.

Paragon S.meta_verdict: B-tier Enus with excellent balance; the Armored's purchasable sibling done properly.

Niobe.meta_verdict: A-tier newcomer that immediately challenged Pariah and Itali RSX at the top of the class.

Coquette D1.meta_verdict: D-tier Coquette D1 — overpriced at $1.5M, trading on the Coquette name without the performance.

Pipistrello.meta_verdict: B-tier with excellent balance; one of the better-handling supers at any price point.

Cavalcade XL.meta_verdict: C-tier Cavalcade XL — the large Escalade-style SUV that trades size for racing relevance.

Baller ST-D.meta_verdict: S-tier Baller that dominates SUV racing — the class benchmark and the obvious first purchase.

Terminus.meta_verdict: A-tier performance at a premium price — the Terminus competes at the very top of Off-Road racing.

Dorado.meta_verdict: D-tier Dorado at $1.375M — mid-pack SUV that doesn't justify the premium price tag.

Impaler SZ.meta_verdict: B-tier Impaler variant with lowrider stance — competitive enough without reaching the top shelf.

Vigero ZX Convertible.meta_verdict: Same A-tier performance as the ZX with the roof gone — equally competitive, twice as stylish.

Impaler LX.meta_verdict: C-tier lowrider Impaler — pays premium price for modest gains over the base model.

Dominator GT.meta_verdict: S-tier Dominator that finally earned the nameplate — top muscle class pick for serious racers.

Vivanite.meta_verdict: B-tier Vivanite — competitive upper-mid SUV with a solid racing pedigree and a fair price.

Aleutian.meta_verdict: E-tier Aleutian at $1.835M — expensive for an E-tier finish; handsome styling can't disguise the tier.

Turismo Omaggio.meta_verdict: A-tier racing with iconic styling — one of the few supers that fully earns its price.

Hotring Hellfire.meta_verdict: Hotring beast that roars on the oval and has no idea what to do elsewhere.

Buffalo EVX.meta_verdict: A-tier electric Bravado that actually works on the racetrack — muscle class's unlikely EV success story.

La Coureuse.meta_verdict: B-tier French sports car with sharp handling; consistently underrated, consistently fast.

Brigham.meta_verdict: J-tier Lampadati luxury coupe lost in the wrong vehicle class — races like it knows it.

Ratel.meta_verdict: C-tier armored buggy with combat potential — solid mid-range pick for mixed off-road use.

MonstroCiti.meta_verdict: C-tier lifted truck that delivers genuine off-road capability without breaking into the top tier.

Clique Wagon.meta_verdict: M-tier station wagon lowrider at $1.205M — genuinely one of GTA's most absurd purchases.

Boor.meta_verdict: G-tier Boor at $1.28M — military jeep aesthetic with G-tier off-road credentials to match.

Eudora.meta_verdict: J-tier luxury muscle sedan — looks expensive, drives expensively slow.

Hotring Everon.meta_verdict: Oval-spec hotring racer that dominates its niche and confuses everywhere else.

Issi Rally.meta_verdict: C-tier Issi Rally — the rally-spec Issi classified as SUV, surprisingly competitive for its size.

Virtue.meta_verdict: Consistently B-tier; the straightforward choice for players who want results, not experiments.

Panthere.meta_verdict: Retro Ferrari Testarossa tribute — stylish mid-tier with a charm that outpaces its lap times.

Broadway.meta_verdict: K-tier stretched luxury lowrider — the most impractical vehicle in the muscle class, somehow purchasable.

Powersurge.meta_verdict: Electric sport bike with genuine racing pedigree — strong alternative to the Carbon RS.

300R.meta_verdict: F-tier Italian GT that looks expensive and races expensively poorly.

Entity MT.meta_verdict: H-tier Overflod that disappoints on track; looks the part, rarely plays it.

Tulip M-100.meta_verdict: F-tier Tulip variant at $1.658M — overpriced for F-tier performance, style doesn't justify the cost.

Tahoma Coupe.meta_verdict: F-tier muscle coupe that tries hard aesthetically and races like it's not trying at all.

Weevil Custom.meta_verdict: A-tier VW Bug tribute in the Muscle class — unexpected, delightful, and genuinely fast.

10F Widebody.meta_verdict: D-tier at $575K — accessible entry for players who want to race without a major investment.

Sentinel Classic Widebody.meta_verdict: D-tier JDM classic with widebody kit; style-to-performance ratio leans heavily toward style.

Vigero ZX.meta_verdict: A-tier Camaro tribute that reinvented the Muscle class — the Dominator's fiercest rival.

SM722.meta_verdict: G-tier Benefactor priced like it belongs in S-tier — overpriced disappointment.

Ruiner ZZ-8.meta_verdict: B-tier Ruiner revival with actual racing legs — the original Ruiner's long-overdue redemption arc.

LM87.meta_verdict: Consistent B-tier with clean handling; reliable across every track type without drama.

Torero XO.meta_verdict: C-tier racer on the iconic Torero silhouette; wins lobbies, not championships.

Omnis e-GT.meta_verdict: Electric crossover experiment stuck at G-tier — impressive tech, unimpressive lap times.

Draugur.meta_verdict: A-tier off-roader that earns its ranking — one of the class's best competitive picks.

Greenwood.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,098,750) unlocks after completing "ULP - Intelligence" (Operation Paper Trail, The Contract DLC).
Greenwood.meta_verdict: C-tier Imani Tech station wagon — the gadgets make it unique, the tier keeps it grounded.

10F.meta_verdict: E-tier Annis with a clean aesthetic but zero competitive upside.

Corsita.meta_verdict: Solid C-tier mid-engined sports; reliable for competitive lobbies at a fair price.

Weaponized Ignus.meta_verdict: A-tier racing performance plus weapons; the only super that does both without compromise.

Cyclone II._note: HSW upgrade ($475,000) available at LS Car Meet — requires PS5/Xbox Series X|S and visiting Hao's Special Works.
Cyclone II.meta_verdict: Electric super with beautiful handling; immediate top-tier from day one.

Astron Custom.meta_verdict: A-tier Astron Custom — the upgraded variant with better performance and an appropriately higher price tag.

Arbiter GT.meta_verdict: E-tier muscle coupe at $1.58M — looks great in the garage, races without distinction.

S95._note: HSW upgrade ($525,000) available at LS Car Meet — requires PS5/Xbox Series X|S.
S95.meta_verdict: Grotti sports coupe with style to spare and a tier position that disappoints.

Granger 3600LX.meta_verdict: F-tier armored Granger 3600LX at $2M — protection over performance; the Escalade of combat SUVs.

I-Wagen.meta_verdict: C-tier I-Wagen at $1.729M — electric SUV with actual racing credentials, just not quite A-tier ones.

Patriot Mil-Spec.meta_verdict: K-tier military Hummer tribute at $1.71M — expensive aesthetic purchase with no competitive merit.

Baller ST.meta_verdict: B-tier Baller ST — strong competitive SUV, one step below the ST-D but still elite class.

Reever.meta_verdict: Pricey sport bike at $1.9M that sits just below the Carbon RS — fast but not the fastest.

Shinobi.meta_verdict: Newer sport bike with top-tier speed — sleek Dinka design that competes with Carbon RS.

Comet S2 Cabrio.meta_verdict: Same F-tier bones as the Comet S2 with the roof off — skip both.

Zeno.meta_verdict: F-tier exotic that looks the part and races average; $2.8M for the disappointment.

Buffalo STX.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades AND trade price ($1,612,500) both require owning an Agency — doubly Agency-dependent.
Buffalo STX.meta_verdict: S-tier Bravado with Imani Tech gadgets — the best car in the class and it's not close.

Ignus.meta_verdict: Late addition to the super class that immediately earned a top-tier slot.

Astron.meta_verdict: A-tier Astron at $1.58M — strong upper-class SUV racer, worth the investment for competitive play.

Champion.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency.
Champion._note: Repriced during Los Santos Drug Wars update (was $2,995,000 / trade $2,246,250 at launch).
Champion.meta_verdict: I-tier racing at $3.75M — the worst value ratio in the entire super class.

Jubilee.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,237,500) unlocks after completing 20 Security Contracts as leader.
Jubilee.meta_verdict: B-tier Jubilee — Imani Tech SUV where the gadgets justify what the tier position doesn't.

Sultan RS Classic.meta_verdict: E-tier rally aesthetic with a classic soul — charming but competitively irrelevant.

Dominator ASP.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted. Trade price ($1,331,250) requires LS Car Meet Reputation points.
Dominator ASP.meta_verdict: A-tier Dominator variant slotting just below the GT — still elite muscle class racing.

Cypher.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency.
Cypher.meta_verdict: G-tier Imani Tech sports car — the gadgets are the only reason to own it.

Growler.meta_verdict: Beautiful E-tier Bravado — buy it for the garage aesthetic, not the podium.

Vectre.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades require owning an Agency. Trade price ($1,338,750) unlocks via LS Car Meet reputation. Trade price ($1,338,750) requires LS Car Meet Reputation.
Vectre.meta_verdict: Imani Tech sports car stuck at E-tier — the gadgets don't compensate for the performance.

Comet S2.meta_verdict: G-tier Porsche mid-gen — outclassed by every Comet successor and most competitors.

Remus.meta_verdict: G/H-tier Karin sports coupe — buy it for the looks, not the times.

Jester RR.meta_verdict: E-tier RR without the widebody kit is still just an E-tier car.

RT3000.meta_verdict: Karin's Supra tribute is actually competent — sits at E-tier where it belongs.

ZR350.meta_verdict: RX-7 tribute that drifts beautifully and races adequately — more style than substance.

Dominator GTT.meta_verdict: B-tier Dominator with extra attitude — strong mid-tier buy at $1.22M.

Euros.meta_verdict: F-tier 911 tribute — beautiful daily driver, outpaced by real competitors.

Calico GTF.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted, but Imani Tech upgrades (remote control, jammer, EMP) require owning an Agency ($2,010,000–$2,830,000 from Dynasty 8 Executive).
Calico GTF.meta_verdict: Sport class monster with Imani Tech as a bonus; best value in its class.

Manchez Scout.meta_note: Basic off-road bike. Cheap option for wilderness missions.
Manchez Scout.meta_verdict: The original scout bike; solid for off-road setups before the Acid Lab era.

Verus.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Verus — off-road utility vehicle with minimal legacy before removal.

Itali RSX.meta_verdict: Best sport class car for most tracks; the safe recommendation for any racer.

Weaponized Dinghy.meta_note: Armed boat. Very situational — only useful for specific water-based missions.
Weaponized Dinghy.meta_verdict: Armed speedboat that holds its own in water-based missions; niche but reliable.

Squaddie.meta_verdict: H-tier Squaddie at $1.13M — military-style SUV that looks tough and races accordingly.

Toreador.meta_note: Submarine car with homing missiles and unlimited boost. Excellent all-rounder for grinding and defense.
Toreador.meta_verdict: Renders Oppressor Mk II missiles irrelevant; best counter-grief car in the game.

Sparrow.meta_note: Required for Cayo Perico scope-out and fast travel within the Kosatka. Unlocks as part of the Kosatka package.
Sparrow.meta_verdict: The Kosatka's essential companion — buy it the same session.

Avisa.meta_verdict: Mini-sub stored in the Kosatka; purpose-built for Cayo Perico preps.

Kurtz 31 Patrol Boat.meta_verdict: Fastest boat in the game — the benchmark for anything water-based.

Longfin.meta_verdict: Sleek speedboat with genuine racing pedigree; the Pariah of the water class.

Annihilator Stealth.unlock_condition_note: Requires a Hangar for storage. Trade price ($2,902,500) unlocks after completing The Cayo Perico Heist as leader using this vehicle as the approach.
Annihilator Stealth.meta_note: Off-radar helicopter. Decent but outclassed by Akula for stealth operations.
Annihilator Stealth.meta_verdict: The Akula's less agile cousin; stealth capable but costs $3.87M for the privilege.

Winky.meta_verdict: O-tier WW2 jeep tribute at $1.1M — wildly overpriced for O-tier; a pure collector's purchase.

Gauntlet Classic Custom.meta_verdict: E-tier Classic Gauntlet with the Custom treatment — old muscle given a fresh but slow life.

Yosemite Rancher.meta_verdict: H-tier Yosemite Rancher — lifted ranch truck with H-tier capability and genuine aesthetic charm.

Peyote Custom.meta_verdict: J-tier Peyote Custom — lowrider VW Bug tribute that makes no apologies for its tier position.

Manana Custom.meta_verdict: J-tier Manana Custom lowrider — the lowrider scene's most overlooked entry, races accordingly.

Beater Dukes.meta_verdict: E-tier junkyard Dukes with a weathered look — charming rust bucket with minimal pace.

Seminole Frontier.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Seminole Frontier — lifted SUV variant removed before it built a fanbase.

Landstalker XL.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Landstalker XL — large family SUV taken with the SAM cull.

Penumbra FF.meta_verdict: Front-wheel-drive F-tier sports car — wrong drivetrain, wrong tier, wrong choice.

Coquette D10.meta_verdict: E-tier Coquette variant — better than the base, still not worth racing seriously.

Tigon.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a solid C-tier contender — removed before players fully appreciated it.

Zhaba.meta_verdict: O-tier amphibious vehicle at $2.4M — crosses land and water equally slowly; pure novelty.

JB 700W.meta_verdict: Weaponized JB 700 with spy gadgets and missiles — pure Bond fantasy, no racing relevance.

Sugoi.meta_verdict: Dinka's JDM throwback sits in the mid-lower tier — fun character, limited ceiling.

Drift Yosemite.meta_verdict: A-tier drift-spec Yosemite that's genuinely fast even in standard racing — surprisingly competitive.

Outlaw.meta_verdict: G-tier Outlaw buggy — decent off-road bones without the performance to compete at the top.

Sultan Classic.meta_verdict: D-tier rally legend with gorgeous looks; the Sultan RS Classic is the faster buy.

Stryder.meta_verdict: Futuristic electric dirt bike at $670K — genuinely capable off-road with a premium price tag.

V-STR.meta_verdict: F-tier Benefactor that promised AMG GT vibes and delivered mediocrity.

Imorgon.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier electric with real merit; quietly one of the better removals.

Furia.meta_verdict: E-tier that looks faster than it is — which summarizes the problem entirely.

Rebla GTS.meta_verdict: A-tier Rebla GTS at $1.175M — AMG GLE-style SUV that earns its racing credentials honestly.

Komoda.meta_verdict: F-tier Obey that can't decide if it wants to be a sports car.

Retinue Mk II.meta_verdict: B-tier Retinue Mk II — meaningfully faster than the base, short of the A-tier leaders.

Everon.meta_verdict: New-gen off-roader from The Chop Shop that immediately earned a top-class racing spot.

Vagrant.meta_verdict: A-tier Vagrant at $2.214M — expensive but genuinely the fastest off-road racer in its tier.

Peyote Gasser.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier retro hot rod — cult following, limited pace, no longer sold.

Dynasty.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Dynasty — Chinese-inspired luxury classic taken alongside the SAM cull.

Hellion.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Hellion dune buggy — capable off-roader taken before it found its niche.

Zion Classic.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Zion Classic — BMW Z8-style Sport Classic gone with the update.

Gauntlet Hellfire.meta_verdict: C-tier Gauntlet with Hellcat energy — loud and fun, not a serious race contender.

Novak.meta_verdict: A-tier Novak at $608K — well-priced competitive SUV that punches at the top of its class.

Zorrusso.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Pegassi that never justified its price; not widely mourned.

Jugular.meta_verdict: C-tier at $1.225M — strong value for the performance level; a sensible sports class pick.

Krieger.meta_verdict: Best overall super car; top speed and handling combined in one package.

Locust.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier at $1.625M — expensive and slow; no competitive loss.

Nebula Turbo.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Nebula Turbo — Soviet-era car design that GTA Online briefly made purchasable.

Emerus.meta_verdict: Podium super that rewards smooth driving; doesn't forgive sloppy lines.

Gauntlet Classic.meta_verdict: G-tier Gauntlet in retro trim — the classic aesthetic is real, the racing potential isn't.

Neo.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Solid C-tier sports that was competitive and stylish — a real loss.

Caracara 4x4.meta_note: Decent off-road vehicle for wilderness missions. Situationally useful.
Caracara 4x4.meta_verdict: Best all-purpose off-roader — fast, stable, and handles every terrain type well.

Issi Sport.meta_note: Small, fast, good for contact missions and racing. Budget-friendly grind support vehicle.
Issi Sport.meta_verdict: Removed June 2023 — tiny and fast; still elite for contact missions if owned.

8F Drafter.meta_verdict: E-tier coupe that looks the part but races like a D student.

Paragon R.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The purchasable base Paragon; C-tier and the one most players actually bought.

S80RR.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier and consistently underrated; its removal hurt the competitive roster.

Thrax.meta_verdict: Sharp B-tier at $2.3M — competitive without being the most expensive option in class.

Deveste Eight.meta_verdict: Criminally underrated super with consistent top-three lap times.

Vamos.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Declasse muscle coupe — clean retro design that ended with the update.

Deviant.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Vapid muscle — aggressive styling with limited follow-through on track.

RC Bandito.meta_verdict: Tiny RC racer gated behind an Arcade; fast, low, and hilariously hard to hit.

Schlagen GT.meta_verdict: E-tier SLS tribute — gorgeous in the garage, forgettable on track.

Tulip.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Declasse muscle — punchy retro styling the SAM cull took too soon.

Itali GTO.meta_verdict: A-tier at $1.965M — exceptional value; one of the best racing buys in the entire sports class.

Clique.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier lowrider muscle — solid before removal, a gap that won't be filled.

Toros.meta_verdict: The Toros delivers S-tier SUV racing at $498K — the best value proposition in the class.

Impaler.meta_verdict: C-tier base Impaler at $331K — serviceable muscle, outclassed by its own LX variant.

Freecrawler.meta_verdict: H-tier Freecrawler at $597K — solid rock crawler aesthetic, H-tier off-road reality.

Menacer.meta_verdict: Armored off-road with a gun; a capable poor man's Nightshark for budget players.

Scramjet.meta_note: Excellent mobility with boost and jump. Good griefing defense and fast travel on land.
Scramjet.meta_verdict: Ground combat with missiles and boost; the Oppressor Mk II for non-flyers.

Oppressor Mk II.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted from Warstock, but weapons and boost require a Terrorbyte with Mobile Workshop. Functionally limited without it.
Oppressor Mk II.meta_note: Best all-around grind vehicle: fast travel, weaponized, griefing defense. Essential for efficient CEO/MC work.
Oppressor Mk II.meta_verdict: Buy this before anything else; it makes every other activity faster.

Terrorbyte.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Nightclub. Warstock listing does not appear without one.
Terrorbyte.meta_note: Enables Client Jobs and Oppressor Mk II weapons/boost upgrade. Central hub for efficient grinding operations.
Terrorbyte.meta_verdict: Useless without the Oppressor Mk II; completely indispensable with it.

Patriot Stretch.meta_verdict: M-tier Patriot Stretch at $611K — the stretched limo Hummer; equally slow, considerably longer.

Pounder Custom.meta_note: Used in MC sell missions. Situationally useful for large-stock sales.
Pounder Custom.meta_verdict: Third large bunker sale vehicle; learn the route or it will cost you.

Mule Custom.meta_note: Used in MC and Bunker sell missions. If you run those businesses, you'll use this.
Mule Custom.meta_verdict: Large crate delivery truck; needed for big Import/Export jobs, nothing more.

Swinger.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Swinger — clean British roadster aesthetic, competitively mid-pack before removal.

Jester Classic.meta_verdict: I-tier Jester Classic — retro Japanese sports car aesthetic that races near the bottom of the class.

Michelli GT.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Michelli GT — Italian grand touring aesthetic, mid-pack at best, gone now.

Cheburek.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Cheburek — GTA's beloved Lada joke car, slow by design and proud of it.

Tyrant.meta_verdict: E-tier Lambo-alike; consistent, and consistently mediocre.

Dominator GTX.meta_verdict: C-tier Dominator variant — decent filler, overshadowed by its better siblings above and below.

Sea Sparrow.meta_verdict: Water-landing helicopter; the Sparrow for players without a Kosatka.

Flash GT.meta_verdict: F-tier flash that never delivers; the name is the best part.

Caracara.meta_verdict: The standard Caracara — a reliable off-road platform before the 4x4 made it obsolete.

Tezeract.meta_verdict: Electric hypercar concept that underperforms on every track; style over substance.

Ellie.meta_verdict: H-tier muscle coupe with retro charm — a beautiful car that races like an H-tier car.

GB200.meta_verdict: Rare rally-spec that never found its moment — a curiosity more than a competitor.

Taipan.meta_verdict: J-tier — near the absolute bottom of the super class; avoid entirely for racing.

Entity XXR.meta_verdict: Beautiful I-tier Overflod in a B-tier price bracket — the definition of style tax.

Fagaloa.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Fagaloa — Samoan car culture representation the SAM cull removed quietly.

Hotring Sabre.meta_verdict: The original hotring racer — legacy status, surpassed by every newer variant.

Comet SR.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was genuinely C-tier competitive — one of the better sports losses.

190z.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier 190z — JDM Datsun tribute the SAM cull took without fanfare.

Hustler.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier hot rod — more style icon than race car, correctly categorized.

Kamacho.meta_verdict: Top-tier off-road racer with exceptional handling on dirt and gravel.

Neon.meta_verdict: Electric Pfister sports car with E-tier performance — the novelty wears off fast.

Revolter.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier electric sports; gone before most players noticed.

GT500.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier GT500 — classic GT racer styling with a tier position that belied the looks.

Viseris.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Viseris — Lotus Esprit tribute gone with the SAM update.

Savestra.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Savestra — Alfa Romeo Spider tribute the cull took quietly.

Autarch.meta_verdict: Solid C-tier that holds its own on most tracks; not flashy, just reliable.

Hermes.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier retro custom — beautiful Art Deco design, missed by lowrider fans.

Streiter.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $500K — bottom performance, mid-tier price; no tears shed.

Avenger.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility (Doomsday Heist property).
Avenger.meta_note: Flying base with weapon workshop. Expensive but versatile. Niche but useful for certain operations.
Avenger.meta_verdict: Flying base with vehicle workshop; overkill for most, essential for Doomsday.

Deluxo.meta_note: Flying car with homing missiles. Expensive and outclassed by Oppressor Mk II, but fun and functional.
Deluxo.meta_verdict: Flying DeLorean with missiles — thrilling to own, terrible value next to the Mk II.

Stromberg.meta_note: Submarine car. Outclassed by Toreador but cheaper. Situational utility.
Stromberg.meta_verdict: Submersible car with torpedoes; the most creative vehicle in its class.

Chernobog.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($1,125,000) unlocks after completing "Escort ULP" setup as heist leader.
Chernobog.meta_note: Massive missile launcher. Fun but impractical; too slow and unwieldy for real grind use.
Chernobog.meta_verdict: Static missile launcher needing a Facility; zero grinding applications, zero excuses.

Barrage.meta_verdict: Military buggy with weapons that excels at controlled chaos across any terrain.

Akula.unlock_condition_note: Requires both a Facility AND a Hangar to purchase and store. Trade price ($3,375,000) unlocks after completing the "Setup: Server Farm" mission as Doomsday Heist leader.
Akula.meta_note: Off-radar capability makes it excellent for avoiding griefers during sell missions. Strong utility.
Akula.meta_verdict: Goes off-radar; that one feature is worth every penny of the price.

SC1.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier through its entire lifespan; no great loss competitively.

Raiden.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier electric; the Neon replaced and outperforms it.

Pariah.meta_verdict: Fastest sports car ever tested — the benchmark everything else is measured against.

Yosemite.meta_verdict: A-tier Karin pickup at $485K — best value proposition in the entire muscle class.

Riata.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Riata — off-road pickup taken by the SAM cull without ceremony.

FH-1 Hunter.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Hangar before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($3,100,000) unlocks after completing 42 Air Freight Cargo source missions as MC President.
FH-1 Hunter.meta_note: Military attack helicopter. Powerful but high skill ceiling and expensive.
FH-1 Hunter.meta_verdict: The best attack helicopter in the game if helicopters are your thing.

Cyclone.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original electric super; the Cyclone II is everything it wasn't.

Vigilante.meta_note: Fast jet-powered car with missiles but no grind utility. Pure fun vehicle.
Vigilante.meta_verdict: Batman car that explodes if anyone looks at it; missiles don't compensate.

Rapid GT Classic.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier but with iconic styling — a GTA Online visual staple since launch.

XLS (Armored).meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier XLS Armored — bullet-resistant SUV taken by the cull, no replacement offered.

XLS.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier XLS — the unarmored base version, outclassed before and after removal.

Serrano.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Serrano — compact SUV that came and went without leaving a mark.

Seminole.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Seminole — the base before the Frontier, gone with both SAM and relevance.

Rocoto.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Rocoto — the Toyota RAV4 tribute that left without fanfare.

Radius.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Radius — budget civilian SUV gone with the SAM cull, barely noticed.

Patriot.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier Patriot — GTA Online's original Hummer tribute, retired after a long run.

Landstalker.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Landstalker — GTA's original large SUV, retired by its own XL successor.

Huntley S.meta_verdict: E-tier Huntley S — aged community favorite, still a bargain at $195K for what you get.

Habanero.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Habanero — spicy name, plain SUV, gone with the update.

Gresley.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Gresley — mid-size SUV that left no legacy behind.

Granger.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier base Granger — the iconic police SUV, gone from stores after SAM.

FQ 2.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier FQ 2 — budget SUV from GTA Online's early era, correctly forgotten.

Contender.meta_verdict: C-tier Contender at $250K — budget-priced SUV that over-delivers for its price point.

Cavalcade.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier original Cavalcade — the Escalade OG, retired after years of loyal service.

BeeJay XL.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier BeeJay XL — oversized luxury SUV parody that raced like it looked.

Baller LE LWB (Armored).meta_verdict: F-tier Baller LWB Armored — bulletproof at the cost of any remaining racing relevance.

Baller LE LWB.meta_verdict: F-tier Baller LWB — long-wheelbase SUV that added length and subtracted lap time.

Baller LE (Armored).meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Baller LE Armored — the armored variant taken alongside the base LE.

Baller LE.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Baller LE — the Luxury Edition that couldn't hold its tier before removal.

Zentorno.meta_verdict: The 2014 meta king — E-tier today but only $725K and a genuine GTA Online icon.

XA-21.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a legitimate C-tier racer — a real loss from the competitive pool.

X80 Proto.meta_verdict: The F1 car that isn't — E-tier performance and a $2.7M price that stings.

Voltic.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier electric oddity; the Cyclone II is its spiritual successor done right.

Visione.meta_verdict: D-tier performance in a gorgeous body — buy it for the garage, not the podium.

Vagner.meta_verdict: B-tier cornering at $1.5M — the best value in the competitive super tier by a wide margin.

Vacca.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Cheapest entry into the super class at $240K; I-tier but accessible.

Tyrus.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Competent E-tier Overflod; gone before anyone got attached.

Tempesta.meta_verdict: D-tier with decent style; outclassed in a fair fight but not embarrassing at $1.3M.

T20.meta_verdict: Was the 2015 benchmark super; a decade of power creep left it E-tier.

Sultan RS.meta_verdict: Rally super that belongs in sports class; I-tier here, surprisingly fun there.

Rocket Voltic.meta_verdict: Rocket-boosted stunt car — not a racer, but the most fun in its lane.

Reaper.meta_verdict: G-tier Lambo lookalike; genuinely beautiful, genuinely disappointing on track.

RE-7B.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was a genuine B-tier contender — one of the bigger losses in the purge.

Penetrator.meta_verdict: G-tier Pegassi that's slow even by super standards; name is the fastest thing about it.

Osiris.meta_verdict: Was revolutionary in 2015; E-tier a decade later tells the whole story.

Nero Custom.meta_verdict: Benny's Nero trades money for marginal E-tier gains; still not competitive.

Nero.meta_verdict: F-tier with excellent aesthetics; the garage trophy, not the race winner.

Itali GTB Custom.meta_verdict: Benny's makes the GTB pricier but not faster enough to matter; skip both.

Itali GTB.meta_verdict: F-tier Italian GT that costs $1.2M; overpriced for the performance, underpowered for the badge.

Infernus.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier but a GTA institution since Vice City — irreplaceable nostalgia.

GP1.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier Progen that never lived up to what the badge implied.

FMJ.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier; succeeded by the FMJ MK V, which is the clear upgrade.

ETR1.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was D-tier and genuinely competitive; a quiet loss in the SAM purge.

Entity XF.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier but one of GTA Online's most iconic supers — genuinely missed.

Cheetah.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier on track, legendary in culture — a Vice City ghost.

Bullet.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Was the cheapest super ever at $155K; K-tier but a GTA IV icon.

Banshee 900R.meta_verdict: I-tier as a super but only $565K; genuinely better compared against sports class.

Adder.meta_verdict: The 2013 prestige car; $1M and tier H make it a collector piece, not a racer.

Z-Type.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Z-Type at $950K — famously overpriced even when it was for sale.

Turismo Classic.meta_verdict: A-tier Ferrari 250 GTO tribute at $705K — outstanding value for the best Sport Classic racing.

Tornado Rat Rod.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Tornado Rat Rod — wild custom hot rod aesthetic, taken with the rest.

Tornado Custom.meta_verdict: N-tier Tornado Custom — lowrider classic that belongs in a car show, not on a circuit.

Tornado.meta_verdict: O-tier Tornado at $30K — the basement of Sport Classics, cheap enough to own without guilt.

Torero.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. B-tier Pegassi Torero — Lamborghini Miura tribute taken by the cull at peak form.

Stinger GT.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Stinger GT — the GT badge didn't move the tier needle before removal.

Stinger.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Stinger — an original GTA classic that quietly exited with the SAM cull.

Roosevelt Valor.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. J-tier Roosevelt Valor — 1930s luxury car with mounted gun, gone with the update.

Roosevelt.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Roosevelt — the 1930s luxury classic, gone before it could build a legacy.

Retinue.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier base Retinue — the foundation for the Mk II, gone before the upgrade culture caught on.

Pigalle.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Pigalle — avant-garde French design that never found its audience in GTA.

Peyote.meta_verdict: N-tier Peyote at $38K — the base before the Custom; buy it as the stepping stone.

Monroe.meta_verdict: G-tier Monroe — elegance over pace; the most beautiful Sport Classic in the game.

Manana.meta_verdict: M-tier Manana at $10K — the cheapest Sport Classic, priced exactly right for what it offers.

Mamba.meta_verdict: H-tier Mamba at $995K — Cobra tribute that looks the part and races near the bottom.

JB 700.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier JB 700 — the spy car with ejector seat, removed but never forgotten.

Infernus Classic.meta_verdict: C-tier Infernus Classic — beautiful Ferrari homage that races honestly at mid-pack.

Fr\u00e4nken Stange.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Fränken Stange — GTA's most unhinged hot rod design, a true one-of-a-kind.

Coquette Classic.meta_verdict: G-tier Coquette Classic — beautiful C1 Corvette tribute that drifts well and races slowly.

Cheetah Classic.meta_verdict: A-tier Ferrari classic tribute that earns its price — genuine Sport Classic racing pedigree.

Casco.meta_verdict: F-tier Casco at $904K — handsome mid-century design that races at the low end of the class.

Ardent.meta_verdict: A-tier Aston Martin tribute with missiles and machine guns — style and violence in one package.

Verlierer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier German sports; distinctive, forgettable in every other way.

Tropos Rallye.meta_verdict: Rally-styled relic that time forgot — zero meta relevance, decent photo subject.

Drift Tampa.meta_verdict: Built for drifting not racing — fun niche toy, zero competitive use.

Surano.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier convertible; a stylish casualty of the purge.

Sultan.meta_verdict: Base Sultan at ~$12K is the best bargain in GTA sports; just don't expect trophies.

Specter Custom.meta_note: Fast sports car with weapons. Decent for missions requiring speed and light firepower.
Specter Custom.meta_verdict: Fast Benny's sport that earns its keep on quick-extract missions.

Specter.meta_verdict: The Specter Custom is already F-tier — this base version has even less excuse.

Seven-70.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Bugatti-adjacent; more show than go, not widely missed.

Schwartzer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier BMW tribute; went quietly and largely unlamented.

Schafter V12.meta_verdict: G-tier luxury sports sedan — drives like a boardroom, not a racetrack.

Schafter LWB.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier luxury sedan in the sports class; always an identity crisis.

Ruston.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $430K; hard to justify even when it was available.

Raptor.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $648K — bottom performance at a mid-tier price.

Rapid GT.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier but a beloved GTA Online staple since day one — genuinely missed.

Penumbra.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier at $24K — the drift car that never got its moment.

Omnis.meta_verdict: Base Omnis is an off-road oddity that barely registers as a sports car.

Massacro (Racecar).meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier track variant of the Massacro; outclassed even in its era.

Massacro.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Ferrari 458 tribute that was beloved before the meta moved on.

Lynx.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Jaguar-alike; not mourned competitively.

Kuruma (Armored).meta_note: Best early-game grind vehicle for contact missions; near-invincible against bullets. Essential before Nightshark.
Kuruma (Armored).meta_verdict: Bulletproof windows for $525K — best early-game purchase by a wide margin.

Kuruma.meta_verdict: The armored Kuruma is the icon — the base version is just a forgettable sports car.

Khamelion.meta_verdict: Forgotten electric concept that never found a player base — lower-tier relic.

Jester (Racecar).meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier track variant; once a popular race build, now a garage relic.

Jester.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier original Jester; foundation of the nameplate, outclassed by all successors.

Futo.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier but the drift community's icon at $9K — culture over lap times.

Fusilade.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $36K; cheap and slow, a forgotten footnote.

Furore GT.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $448K — wildly overpriced for the performance even before removal.

Feltzer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Mercedes SLR — a longtime SSASA staple removed in the purge.

Elegy RH8.meta_verdict: Free from Social Club link and still D-tier competitive; a cornerstone of the sports class for a decade.

Elegy Retro Custom.meta_verdict: Former free Social Club reward now rarely seen — E-tier legacy piece.

Coquette.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier but a GTA V launch icon — the Corvette that started many careers.

Comet Retro Custom.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier Benny's Comet; overpriced even when available.

Comet.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original F-tier Comet; better variants exist and replaced it.

Carbonizzare.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Ferrari California tribute at H-tier; soft-top and softly forgotten.

Sprunk Buffalo.meta_verdict: Sprunk-branded Buffalo is a collector's flex and nothing more competitively.

Buffalo S.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier track Buffalo; an enduring muscle-sports hybrid, now gone.

Buffalo.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier at $35K — the sports Buffalo; cheap, cheerful, and forgotten.

Go Go Monkey Blista._note: Free — requires Arena War Skill level 20. No GTA$ price.
Go Go Monkey Blista.unlock_condition_note: Free at SSASA after reaching Arena War Skill rank 20 (separate progression track from regular RP).
Go Go Monkey Blista.meta_verdict: Free community gift that barely registers as a sports car — charming, trivially slow.

Bestia GTS.meta_verdict: F-tier Pegassi sports car — decent enough but overshadowed by everything above it.

Banshee.meta_verdict: The base Banshee is an H-tier relic — the 900R upgrade is what you actually want.

Alpha.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier at $150K — cheap enough that nobody mourned it much.

9F Cabrio.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Convertible 9F; same tier, same fate.

9F.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier 2013 Audi R8 tribute; the entire sports class passed it by.

Luxor Deluxe.meta_note: Gold-plated status symbol. Zero grinding utility. Pure flex.
Luxor Deluxe.meta_verdict: $10M private jet that flies exactly like the $1.1M Shamal.

Hydra.meta_note: Outclassed by other aircraft for every grind purpose. High cost, low return for grinding.
Hydra.meta_verdict: Expensive VTOL jet with a steep learning curve; Oppressor Mk II does this job better.

Trophy Truck.meta_verdict: D-tier Trophy Truck at $550K — the name suggests Baja racing; the tier suggests otherwise.

Technical Custom.meta_verdict: The Technical Custom at $142.5K is the best bang-for-buck armed transport in the game.

Technical Aqua.meta_verdict: Amphibious Technical with mounted gun — crosses water and fights; surprisingly practical for its price.

Technical.meta_verdict: Armed Technical pickup at $1.263M — basic mounted gun transport, outclassed by its own Custom version.

Street Blazer.meta_verdict: B-tier Street Blazer ATV at $81K — the best value off-road racer in the entire class.

Sandking XL.meta_verdict: The classic overland truck; removed from stores but a GTA Online institution.

Sandking SWB.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. L-tier Sandking SWB — the shorter, lesser Sandking taken alongside the XL.

Rusty Rebel.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Rusty Rebel at $3K — GTA Online's cheapest off-roader, correctly priced, now gone.

Rebel.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier Rebel pickup — basic off-road truck gone quietly with the SAM cull.

Rancher XL.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Rancher XL — old pickup truck the SAM cull caught on its way out.

Ramp Buggy.meta_verdict: Ramp Buggy at $3.192M — exists solely to flip other vehicles; GTA's greatest trolling investment.

Nightshark.meta_note: Near-invincible armored SUV with missile resistance. Top defensive vehicle for missions and sales.
Nightshark.meta_verdict: Bulletproof and nearly missile-proof; the definitive hostile-delivery vehicle.

Marshall.meta_verdict: The monster truck — drives over everything, including common sense.

Kalahari.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. M-tier Kalahari — basic rally buggy that never distinguished itself before removal.

Insurgent.meta_verdict: The base Insurgent is I-tier and unseated — the Pick-Up Custom is always the right call.

Injection.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Injection buggy — the starter off-roader that barely made it to retirement.

Hot Rod Blazer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Hot Rod Blazer ATV — the custom Blazer variant taken with the cull.

Dune FAV.meta_verdict: Military dune buggy — low-profile, fast, and great for open-terrain missions.

Dune Buggy.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Dune Buggy at $20K — the OG off-road classic, quietly removed.

Dubsta 6x6.meta_verdict: Six-wheel-drive monster that climbs anything; removed from stores but iconic.

Desert Raid.meta_verdict: D-tier Desert Raid at $695K — capable enough off-road, not competitive at the top.

Brawler.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. F-tier Brawler — rugged off-roader with genuine capability, taken by the SAM cull.

Bodhi.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Bodhi — GTA Online's classic jeep, old and beloved before its removal.

Blazer Lifeguard.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. N-tier Blazer Lifeguard — lifeguard-liveried ATV that served no competitive purpose.

Blazer Aqua.meta_verdict: Amphibious ATV at $1.755M — crosses water where others can't; overpriced for what it actually does.

Blazer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier base Blazer ATV — the cheapest off-roader that ever existed in GTA Online.

Bifta.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. E-tier Bifta dune buggy — light, nimble, gone with the update.

Voodoo Custom.meta_verdict: N-tier Voodoo with Custom kit — still N-tier, now with hydraulics and style.

Voodoo.meta_verdict: N-tier lowrider at $5.5K — the cheapest lowrider in the game, priced exactly right.

Virgo Classic Custom.meta_verdict: I-tier Virgo with Custom treatment — luxury liner with I-tier racing credentials to match.

Virgo Classic.meta_verdict: N-tier Virgo Classic — a rolling piece of cruising culture with zero racing ambition.

Virgo.meta_verdict: K-tier classic lowrider — deep in the bottom tier but genuinely rich in character.

Vigero.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. H-tier classic Vigero — the ZX's ancestor, retired alongside the update.

Weaponized Tampa.meta_note: Multiple weapon hardpoints. Good support vehicle for missions.
Weaponized Tampa.meta_verdict: Four-barrel minigun on a muscle car; fun in missions, awkward everywhere else.

Tampa.meta_verdict: F-tier base Tampa for $375K — save those dollars for the Weaponized version instead.

Burger Shot Stallion.meta_verdict: F-tier Stallion with fast food branding — the worst of both worlds in one vehicle.

Stallion.meta_verdict: G-tier Stallion at $71K — cheap muscle class entry that delivers exactly what it costs.

Slamvan Custom.meta_verdict: E-tier Slamvan with custom stance — more show than go, but the show is genuinely good.

Slamvan.meta_verdict: O-tier base Slamvan — buy this only as the stepping stone to the Slamvan Custom.

Sabre Turbo Custom.meta_verdict: B-tier classic muscle with the Custom treatment — old school with just enough modern pace.

Sabre Turbo.meta_verdict: D-tier base Sabre at $15K — the Custom version is worth it, this one isn't.

Ruiner 2000.meta_note: Cool concept but impractical for grinding. Outclassed by Oppressor Mk II in every measurable way.
Ruiner 2000.meta_verdict: Bond car cosplay at $5.7M; the missions it unlocks don't come close to justifying it.

Ruiner.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Firebird homage — iconic hood art, minimal racing merit, now gone.

Rat-Truck.meta_verdict: O-tier rat rod at $37.5K — the very bottom of Muscle class, still somehow loveable.

Rat-Loader.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. O-tier Rat-Loader ratrod — aesthetic outlived its store listing.

Picador.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. I-tier Picador ute — a curiosity that few mourned, taken by SAM anyway.

Nightshade.meta_verdict: H-tier Vapid muscle coupe — neat design that sits at the bottom of relevant racing.

Moonbeam Custom.meta_verdict: I-tier Moonbeam minivan Custom — a minivan in the muscle class is peak GTA absurdity.

Moonbeam.meta_verdict: M-tier base Moonbeam minivan at $32.5K — a minivan, full stop, nothing more to say.

Lurcher.meta_verdict: H-tier Lurcher hearse — Halloween aesthetic with lap times to match the horror.

Hotknife.meta_verdict: L-tier hot rod at $90K — pure aesthetics, zero lap times, no apologies.

Redwood Gauntlet.meta_verdict: F-tier Gauntlet in racing livery that lied about its ambitions on track.

Gauntlet.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. G-tier classic Gauntlet — the muscle class OG, long past its competitive prime.

Faction Custom Donk.meta_verdict: I-tier Donk-spec Faction — the most eye-catching vehicle in the class, the worst racer.

Faction.meta_verdict: I-tier base Faction at $36K — buy it only as the stepping stone to the Custom.

Dukes.meta_verdict: D-tier Charger tribute at $62K — slow, old, nostalgic, and somehow still on the lot.

Duke O'Death.meta_note: Free for returning players. Bulletproof cabin, great for early grinding. Best free armored car.
Duke O'Death.meta_verdict: Free for returning players and better than most $2M muscle cars.

Dominator.meta_verdict: The base Dominator at $35K is C-tier bargain muscle — functional, cheap, nothing special.

Coquette BlackFin.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier Coquette oddly filed under Muscle — misclassified and correctly removed.

Chino Custom.meta_verdict: L-tier Chino with the Custom kit — more style, same slow.

Chino.meta_verdict: L-tier Chino lowrider — pure cruise culture, zero race culture.

Buccaneer Custom.meta_verdict: H-tier Buccaneer with custom lowrider treatment — looks dramatically better, races no differently.

Buccaneer.meta_verdict: H-tier Vapid classic at $29K — bottom of the barrel, but it costs almost nothing.

Blade.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. D-tier classic chopper — cult following couldn't save it from the cull.

Zombie Chopper.meta_verdict: The Zombie Chopper survived SAM while its Bobber sibling didn't — both are equally slow.

Zombie Bobber.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC bobber — stylish zombie aesthetic, average performance, now gone.

Wolfsbane.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — outlaw aesthetic the SAM cull took quietly.

Vortex.meta_verdict: Futuristic Pegassi concept bike at $356K — striking design that doesn't translate to meaningful performance.

Vindicator.meta_verdict: Heavyweight cruiser at $630K — buy it for the presence, not the lap times.

Vader.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Budget commuter bike from GTA Online's early days — gone, rarely mourned.

Thrust.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Sport bike with decent speed — removed before it could build a following.

Sovereign.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Harley-style cruiser — pure aesthetics, no competitive legacy to miss.

Shotaro.meta_verdict: Tron-inspired light cycle that's genuinely fast — best-looking bike with real racing credentials.

Sanctus.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Skull-decorated chopper at $1.995M — wildly overpriced even before removal.

Sanchez (Livery).meta_verdict: Livery Sanchez at $7K — the OG dirt bike's purchasable survivor, charmingly slow.

Sanchez.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The original dirt bike — a GTA icon retired by the update.

Ruffian.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Budget sport bike that never threatened the top spots — quietly removed.

Rat Bike.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Junkyard aesthetic chopper — more character than performance, correctly removed.

PCJ 600.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. GTA's OG stunt bike — legendary in story mode, barely relevant in Online.

Oppressor.meta_note: Mk I rocket bike; useful but requires Mk II for full potential. Decent mid-tier grind option.
Oppressor.meta_verdict: The original hoverboard — fun relic, completely outclassed by the Mk II.

Nightblade.meta_verdict: Dark chopper with a solid aesthetic — doesn't race and doesn't pretend to.

Nemesis.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Low-spec sport bike from the early era — long outclassed and now gone.

Manchez.meta_verdict: Base Manchez at $67K — the stepping stone to the Scout, not a destination itself.

Lectro.meta_verdict: Electric sport bike at $997.5K — fast for its era, outpaced by newer additions.

Innovation.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Old cruiser that never innovated enough — the name wrote the review.

Hexer.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — Lost MC aesthetic, taken by the SAM cull.

Hakuchou.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The base before the Drag — solid sport bike power-crept by its own Custom version.

Gargoyle.meta_verdict: Off-road dirt bike that rivals the BF400 — capable alternative at a similar price point.

FCR 1000 Custom.meta_verdict: The best-looking cruiser in the game with appropriately slow lap times — buy it for the garage.

FCR 1000.meta_verdict: Base FCR 1000 — buy the Custom version instead; this is just the stepping stone.

Faggio Sport.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. The fastest moped in GTA history — that's the ceiling, and it was enough.

Faggio Mod.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Modded Faggio moped — slightly faster, equally unmissable.

Faggio.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. GTA's iconic moped — slow, beloved, appropriately retired.

Esskey.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Electric delivery scooter at $264K — a novelty purchase that nobody needed.

Enduro.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Dirt bike that predated the BF400 — the BF400 made it irrelevant first.

Double-T.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Low-tier sport bike from GTA Online's early era — long forgotten.

Diabolus Custom.meta_verdict: Electric Diabolus Custom with boost ability — fun novelty bike with a genuine trick up its sleeve.

Diabolus.meta_verdict: Base Diabolus electric bike — buy the Custom version instead; this is just the foundation.

Defiler.meta_verdict: Big bagger-style touring bike at $412K — aggressive look, underwhelming pace.

Cliffhanger.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Dirt bike that carved its niche — off-road capable, store listing gone.

Chimera.meta_verdict: Scrambler-style bike at $210K — capable off-road, forgettable on pavement.

Carbon RS.meta_verdict: The fastest non-drag motorcycle available — if you're racing on two wheels, this is it.

BF400.meta_verdict: The BF400 at $95K is the go-to off-road motorcycle — cheap, capable, and universally used.

Bati 801RR.meta_note: Fast motorcycle for contact missions. Good early-game option before Oppressor Mk II.
Bati 801RR.meta_verdict: Removed from stores June 2023 — if you have it, it's still elite for setups.

Bati 801.meta_note: Fast budget motorcycle for missions. Good early-game option.
Bati 801.meta_verdict: The slower Bati; useful for setups, strictly second to the 801RR.

Bagger.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Touring cruiser with zero racing intent — correctly filed under removed.

Avarus.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Biker DLC chopper — solid custom aesthetic, gone with the update.

Akuma.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Fast sport bike in its era — outpaced by every modern motorcycle before removal.

Rhino Tank.meta_note: Classic tank. Great for chaos, poor for efficiency. Fun but not a grind staple.
Rhino Tank.meta_verdict: The classic GTA tank — slow, durable, still the most recognizable vehicle in the game.

Half-track.meta_note: Heavily armored with turret. Good for sell missions with a crew.
Half-track.meta_verdict: Armored quad-cannon truck; decent for sales, no pretense of being subtle.

Valkyrie.meta_note: Attack helicopter with side gunners. Good for co-op but outclassed by Akula solo.
Valkyrie.meta_verdict: The old meta helicopter gunship — still fearsome, now mostly outclassed.

Savage.meta_note: Powerful attack helicopter but too expensive for what it offers. Better options exist at lower cost.
Savage.meta_verdict: Big attack helicopter that costs more and performs worse than the Buzzard Attack Chopper.

Cargobob.meta_note: Required for certain sell missions (vehicle cargo). Invaluable when needed.
Cargobob.meta_verdict: Import/Export large vehicles without this is a nightmare; with it, easy money.

Buzzard Attack Chopper.meta_note: Best budget grind vehicle before Oppressor Mk II. Fast, weaponized, spawns near you as CEO. Excellent early-game.
Buzzard Attack Chopper.meta_verdict: Seven years later, still the best helicopter for making money.

Phantom Custom.meta_note: Required for Phantom Wedge sell missions. Situationally essential.
Phantom Custom.meta_verdict: Large bunker sale vehicle; painful solo, worth owning in a grinding crew.

Hauler Custom.meta_note: Used in certain Gunrunning sell missions. Situationally useful.
Hauler Custom.meta_verdict: Same as Phantom Custom — a crew vehicle masquerading as a solo one.

Police Predator.meta_verdict: Fast police boat recently made purchasable; great for water patrol roleplay and racing.

Kraken.meta_verdict: The submersible submarine — more novelty than utility, but unforgettable to own.

Walton L35 Stock._note: GTABase lists as "Walton L35 (Stock)" — name normalized to match DurtyFree
Walton L35 Stock.meta_verdict: H-tier Stock Walton — unmodified version of the L35, slower and somehow pricier.

Daemon Custom._note: GTABase lists as "Daemon (Custom)" — name normalized to match DurtyFree
Daemon Custom.meta_verdict: Biker DLC custom chopper — pure outlaw aesthetic with zero competitive racing application.

Buzzard._note: Buzzard Attack Chopper variant — base Buzzard is a CEO perk vehicle, not purchasable
Buzzard.meta_note: Useful attack helicopter but no personal spawn perk without CEO. Step down from Buzzard Attack Chopper.
Buzzard.meta_verdict: The missile-less Buzzard; fine for fast travel, not for combat.

Valkyrie MOD.0._note: Appears as NPC in Cayo Perico and Superdollar Deal heist missions. No confirmed purchasable listing — stores entry removed pending verification.

Insurgent Pick-Up Custom.meta_note: Weaponized, heavily armored, team-friendly. Excellent for co-op sell missions.
Insurgent Pick-Up Custom.meta_verdict: The Kuruma's bigger, meaner cousin — absorbs everything and keeps moving.

Kosatka._note: Unlock gate: must meet Miguel Madrazo at The Music Locker first.
Kosatka.unlock_condition_note: Warstock listing unlocks after meeting Miguel Madrazo at The Music Locker (triggered automatically once player reaches sufficient GTA Online progression).
Kosatka.meta_note: Enables Cayo Perico Heist, the best solo money-maker at $1.5M+/hour; the single most important grinding purchase in the game.
Kosatka.meta_verdict: The best solo grind vehicle in the game, period.

Speedo Custom.unlock_condition_note: Bundled free with any Nightclub purchase (After Hours 2018). Not sold separately.
Speedo Custom._note: Price is $0 — free with Nightclub property.
Speedo Custom.meta_note: Required for MC Cocaine/Meth/Cash sell missions. If you run MC businesses, you'll use this.
Speedo Custom.meta_verdict: Comes free with your Nightclub and earns its keep every single sale mission.

Mobile Operations Center.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Bunker. Also serves as the upgrade bay for Mk II weapons.
Mobile Operations Center.meta_note: Research and weapon customization hub. Required for some vehicle upgrades but very slow.
Mobile Operations Center.meta_verdict: The Bunker's command trailer — essential Gunrunning infrastructure, not a combat tool.

Insurgent Pick-Up.meta_verdict: The base Insurgent Pick-Up is functional armed transport — upgrade to the Custom for real capability.

TM-02 Khanjali.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility before Warstock listing appears. Trade price ($2,895,000) unlocks after completing the "Khanjali" setup mission as heist leader.
TM-02 Khanjali.meta_note: Futuristic tank. Slow and cumbersome. Outclassed for grinding; mainly a fun/novelty vehicle.
TM-02 Khanjali.meta_verdict: Facility-gated tank that contributes nothing to any income loop.

APC.unlock_condition_note: Purchase unrestricted from Warstock. Trade price ($2,325,000) unlocks after completing "Offshore Assets" Mobile Operations mission as leader.
APC.meta_note: Armored personnel carrier. Useful but slow and expensive for what it offers.
APC.meta_verdict: Amphibious armored cannon; surprisingly capable, too slow for serious grinding.

Brickade 6x6.unlock_condition_note: Complete the full First Dose + Last Dose mission chains (12 missions total). Then purchase from The Freakshop for $2,095,000.
Brickade 6x6.meta_note: Required for Acid Lab business, one of the best passive income sources. Essential for that grind path.
Brickade 6x6.meta_verdict: The best passive income vehicle added since Cayo Perico.

Hakuchou Drag.meta_verdict: The drag racing king — and more competitive in standard races than it looks.

Mesa.meta_verdict: Removed military Mesa with no price or tier — gone from stores, barely in the records.

Stanier LE Cruiser._note: Chop Shop DLC — price missing from pipeline, sourced from GTA Wiki

Buffalo Cruiser._note: A Safehouse in the Hills DLC — price missing from pipeline, sourced from GTA Wiki

X-treme._note: A Safehouse in the Hills DLC — price missing from pipeline, sourced from GTA Wiki
X-treme.meta_verdict: Off-road capable super; unique in class but limited on tarmac where supers live.

LSCM Jester RR._note: Agents of Sabotage DLC — confirmed purchasable on GTA Wiki, $2,290,000 from Legendary Motorsport
LSCM Jester RR.meta_verdict: Car Meet-exclusive prize vehicle — a flex for lucky winners, no grind value.

Blista._note: Not for sale in GTA Online (sells for $1,600 at LSC only).

Invade and Persuade Tank.meta_note: RC toy tank. No practical grinding value whatsoever.
Invade and Persuade Tank.meta_verdict: Fun RC toy; the Arcade is worth owning, but not for this specifically.

Thruster.unlock_condition_note: Requires owning a Facility. Trade price ($1,875,000) unlocks after completing The Doomsday Scenario as Heist Leader (final act of Doomsday Heist).
Thruster.meta_note: Jetpack with weapons. Niche utility, expensive to unlock, outclassed by Oppressor Mk II.
Thruster.meta_verdict: Jetpack with missiles — the most fun Facility vehicle, the least practical.

Apocalypse Deathbike.meta_verdict: Arena-only Deathbike with post-apoc weaponry — the most feared Arena motorcycle variant.

Future Shock Deathbike.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Deathbike — sci-fi armored motorcycle locked behind Arena buy-in.

Nightmare Deathbike.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Deathbike — bone-decorated monster motorcycle that never leaves the Arena circuit.

Apocalypse Dominator.meta_verdict: Arena-only Dominator with post-apoc weaponry — fully gated behind Arena Points and the Workshop.

Apocalypse Impaler.meta_verdict: Arena-only Impaler with wrecking attachments — Mad Max energy locked behind Arena buy-in.

Apocalypse Imperator.meta_verdict: Arena-only Imperator — the most visually aggressive Arena muscle, appropriately expensive to unlock.

Apocalypse Slamvan.meta_verdict: Arena-only weaponized Slamvan — junkyard monster you'll never see outside Arena War events.

Faction Custom 1._note: Benny's conversion: ~$371K total ($36K base Faction + $335K upgrade). Not a direct purchase price.
Faction Custom 1.meta_verdict: H-tier Faction Custom — the lowrider aesthetic is the entire point; racing is an afterthought.

Future Shock Dominator.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Dominator with sci-fi kit — same closed ecosystem as every Arena variant.

Future Shock Impaler.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Impaler — cool concept locked behind Arena Points and dedicated workshop space.

Future Shock Imperator.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Imperator — the tech-forward muscle pick for Arena sci-fi fans.

Future Shock Slamvan.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Slamvan — weaponized van with a science fiction makeover, Arena-only.

Lost Slamvan.meta_verdict: L-tier Lost MC Slamvan with no listed price — special acquisition only, race value to match.

Nightmare Dominator.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Dominator — skull-faced horror muscle locked in the Arena ecosystem.

Nightmare Impaler.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Impaler with monster truck energy — bone-covered spectacle over substance.

Nightmare Imperator.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Imperator — the spookiest muscle car you can't meaningfully use outside Arena.

Nightmare Slamvan.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Slamvan — bone-festooned weaponized van that never leaves the Arena War circuit.

Phoenix._note: Street spawn only — not sold in GTA Online.
Phoenix.meta_verdict: G-tier Phoenix with no listed price — iconic GTA nameplate running on legacy fumes.

Pisswasser Dominator._note: Free since July 2021 (was $315,000). Store confirmed on SSASA.
Pisswasser Dominator.meta_verdict: Special-livery B-tier Dominator — same race performance as the GTT with sponsored beer branding.

Apocalypse Bruiser.meta_verdict: Arena-only Bruiser with post-apoc weaponry — gated behind Arena Points and the Workshop.

Apocalypse Brutus.meta_verdict: Arena-only Brutus with destruction-spec armaments — fully locked in the Arena ecosystem.

Apocalypse Sasquatch.meta_verdict: Arena-only Sasquatch monster truck — the most chaotic Arena vehicle, appropriately gated.

Duneloader.meta_verdict: O-tier Duneloader farm truck with no listed price — bottom of Off-Road and proud of it.

Future Shock Bruiser.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Bruiser — sci-fi off-road monster locked behind Arena buy-in.

Future Shock Brutus.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Brutus — armored Arena beast with a science fiction makeover.

Future Shock Sasquatch.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena Sasquatch — monster truck with tech upgrades, Arena ecosystem only.

Monster.meta_verdict: L-tier Monster truck with no listed price — special acquisition that wins at spectacle and nothing else.

Nightmare Bruiser.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Bruiser — bone-covered off-road monster that never leaves the Arena circuit.

Nightmare Brutus.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Brutus — horror-themed Arena armored vehicle locked behind Points.

Nightmare Sasquatch.meta_verdict: Nightmare Arena Sasquatch — the spookiest monster truck in GTA, appropriately Arena-gated.

Space Docker.meta_verdict: Alien-themed Space Docker with no listed price — a collectible curiosity from GTA's stranger missions.

Walton L35.meta_verdict: F-tier lifted Walton at $1.67M — heavily overpriced for F-tier off-road performance.

BR8.meta_verdict: Open-wheel racing at its purest — unforgiving but blindingly fast.

PR4.meta_verdict: Fastest open-wheel car; pure racing pedigree in every single stat.

Apocalypse ZR380.meta_verdict: Arena-only ZR380 variant built for spectacle over substance — Arena Points required.

Future Shock ZR380.meta_verdict: Futuristic Arena ZR380 with the same story as every Arena variant — cool concept, closed ecosystem.

Itali GTO Stinger TT.meta_verdict: D-tier at $2.38M — you're paying for the name; the performance doesn't justify the premium.

Jester RR Widebody.meta_verdict: D-tier widebody that looks the part; slightly better than the base RR but the gap is narrow.

Nightmare ZR380.meta_verdict: Nightmare-liveried Arena ZR380 — the best aesthetic of the three, same Arena-only limitations.

Paragon R (Armored)._note: Free — complete all 6 Casino Story Missions as host, or Lucky Wheel prize.
Paragon R (Armored).unlock_condition_note: Complete all 6 Diamond Casino Story Missions as host → collected from Port of Los Santos. Also available as Lucky Wheel podium prize.
Paragon R (Armored).meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Armored Paragon R — bulletproof prestige that's now unobtainable.

Feltzer Classic._note: Internally the Stirling GT, renamed before release (internalName: feltzer3).
Feltzer Classic.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. C-tier Stirling GT tribute — Mercedes 300SL homage taken before it found its audience.

Speedo._note: Showroom rotation vehicle at PDM — may not be available at all times.

Veto Classic.meta_verdict: LSCM race-series exclusive locked to Car Meet events — nowhere else, ever.

Veto Modern.meta_verdict: Modern LSCM racer that you'll never use outside Car Meet events — niche by design.

Burrito (Bail Enforcement).unlock_condition_note: Bundled free with any Bail Office purchase (Maze Bank Foreclosures). Not sold separately.
Burrito (Bail Enforcement)._note: Price is $0 — free with Bail Office property.

Lifeguard._note: Removed from Warstock Cache & Carry in the San Andreas Mercenaries update (June 2023). Was $865,000. No longer purchasable.
Lifeguard._removal_note: Removed from Warstock Cache & Carry in San Andreas Mercenaries update (June 2023).

Daemon._note: Street spawn — not sold in GTA Online.
Daemon.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. Old chopper with no listed price — likely mission-locked, now doubly inaccessible.

Manchez Scout C.unlock_condition_note: Free — bundled with the Brickade 6x6 once purchased and upgraded at The Freakshop. Same First Dose + Last Dose gate.
Manchez Scout C.meta_note: Used in MC sell missions. Cheap and functional for that specific use case.
Manchez Scout C.meta_verdict: Free with the Brickade 6x6 and genuinely earns its keep in Acid Lab preps.

Rampant Rocket.meta_verdict: Rocket-style sport bike at $925K — fast, distinctive, and competitively relevant in motorcycle racing.

Sanchez (livery).meta_verdict: Livery Sanchez at $7K — the OG dirt bike's purchasable survivor, charmingly slow.

Baller._note: Removed from SSASA in San Andreas Mercenaries update (June 2023). Was $90,000.
Baller._removal_note: Removed from Southern SA Super Autos in San Andreas Mercenaries update (June 2023).
Baller.meta_verdict: Removed SAM 2023. K-tier base Baller — the original luxury SUV that launched an entire lineage.

Dubsta._note: Event-only: briefly available at PDM during Junk Energy Skydives Week. Not in standard rotation.
Dubsta.meta_verdict: H-tier Dubsta with no listed price — G-Wagon-style SUV with special acquisition and H-tier results.
